subject: Accounting
title: The Fraud Triangle
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Fraud Triangle usable.

hook.text: Why do honest people commit fraud? Criminologist Donald Cressey found three ingredients are needed.

buildup.text: The fraud triangle has three sides: pressure, opportunity, and rationalization.

discovery.text: Pressure is the motive—debt, lifestyle, or gambling. Opportunity is weak controls. Rationalization is the excuse.

twist.text: Remove any one side and fraud becomes much less likely. Strong internal controls cut opportunity.

climax.text: Forensic accountants study the triangle to predict where fraud is most likely to occur.

punchline.text: Pressure plus opportunity plus excuses equals fraud.

quiz.question: What are the three elements of the fraud triangle?
